new_classes.label_en: Liqueur or cordial in containers > 2 L (subheading 2208 70 90)
new_classes.label_de: Likör oder Cordial in Behältern > 2 L (Unterposition 2208 70 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Liqueur and cordial as described in heading 2208, being a compounded spirituous beverage containing ethyl alcohol or distillate with added sweetening matter and flavourings, presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 70 90.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22087090 identifies liqueurs and cordials as defined in HS EN to heading 2208, first paragraph (B) and third paragraph — i.e. compounded spirituous beverages with sweetening matter and flavourings. The primary distinguishing criterion between 2208 70 10 (small containers ≤ 2 L) and 2208 70 90 (large containers > 2 L) is the container volume threshold. This is captured via the BFO parthood restriction to LargeContainer (≥ 2 L). The SweetenedSpiritQuality and LiqueurProductionProcess restrictions model the product nature of liqueurs/cordials. A small coverage gap remains because the HS Explanatory Note referenced (first paragraph B and third paragraph) contains additional detail about the precise definition of liqueurs vs. cordials (e.g. minimum sugar content thresholds) which cannot be fully expressed without introducing new datatype properties for sugar content — these are not in the current TBox and would require additional axioms. The disjointness complement restriction between the large and small container subclasses is approximated as a restriction on the large container class, but a fully formal owl:disjointWith axiom between sibling classes would require a complement restriction on the class level rather than on an individual restriction.
